description_long: This temple is a rectangular stone structure elevated on a broad platform, accessed by two symmetrical staircases leading to a columned portico. The roof is a pitched gable style, covered with reddish tiles, and the pediment features painted decorative motifs. The exterior walls are finished in a combination of white, gray, and red stone or plaster, with the red band running horizontally along the lower section. The entrance is marked by four prominent columns supporting the portico, and the steps and platform are constructed from large, light-colored stone blocks. The overall design is simple yet monumental, with minimal ornamentation except for the painted pediment and the use of color bands on the walls.
description: A rectangular stone temple with a gabled tiled roof, columned portico, and painted pediment, elevated on a broad platform with twin staircases. The exterior features white, gray, and red stonework with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin staircases, columned portico, and painted pediment above the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the long, plain wall with horizontal red and gray bands and the tiled gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with minimal decoration and the continuation of the gabled roof and platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the plain wall, color bands, and side stair access to the platform.
attribute_ornamentation: painted pediment with geometric and possibly floral motifs, minimal exterior decoration, color banding on walls
attribute_entrance_design: Columned portico with four stone columns, wide central stairway flanked by a secondary stair, and a painted triangular pediment above the entrance
